items.title: Lindsey Vonn Revisits the One-Sleeve Gown in Sequined Gucci at the 2026 ESPY Awards
items.summary: Olympic skier Lindsey Vonn, appearing as a presenter at the ESPYs in New York on July 15, wore a black sequined one-sleeve Gucci column gown with an asymmetrical open shoulder and back, styled with Fred Leighton and Kwiat diamonds. WWD notes the same Gucci design was previously worn by Aryna Sabalenka and Paris Hilton earlier in 2026, underscoring its repeat-red-carpet status.

items.title: Gucci Beauty License Shift to L'Oreal: Analysts Weigh Impact on Coty's Future
items.summary: Wall Street analysts split on the early Gucci Beauty license handover to L'Oreal (effective mid-2027): Jefferies called Coty's $400 million exit payment a favorable resolution of prior uncertainty, while Barclays warned it does not solve Coty's roughly $115 million EBITDA gap once L'Oreal assumes the license, cutting FY28 EBITDA estimates by 32 percent.

items.title: Coty Is Up 10.7% After Handing Back Gucci Beauty License to Kering for US$400 Million
items.summary: Coty shares initially jumped nearly 11 percent on news of the $400 million early exit from the Gucci Beauty license (before later giving back gains and closing down 4 percent), as investors weighed the cash injection for debt reduction against the loss of a marquee luxury license representing meaningful long-term EBITDA.

items.title: Coty to End Gucci Beauty License, Redeploy Capital
items.summary: Coty confirmed the Gucci Beauty license will end June 30, 2027 (one year early), receiving $250 million at signing and up to $150 million more by September 2027. Canaccord Genuity's Susan Anderson maintained a Hold rating, calling the near-term cash benefit clear but the long-term growth impact of losing Gucci 'uncertain.'

items.title: Luxury Giant to Close More Than 200 Stores After Sales Drop
items.summary: Coverage of Kering's plan to shutter roughly 175-200 underperforming stores across its house portfolio, with Gucci absorbing close to a third of the closures as part of CEO Luca de Meo's shift from a volume model toward an exclusivity-and-density strategy; a meaningful share of the cuts land in Asia but US mall/outlet locations are also under review.

items.title: PurseForum Thread: Gucci Price Increase
items.summary: Active PurseForum thread where members trade notes on recent Gucci price hikes, including the Marmont matelasse small shoulder bag's roughly 75 percent increase framed by the brand as an 'elevation strategy.' Posters describe reconsidering Gucci purchases in favor of vintage or resale alternatives given affordability concerns; forum could not be fully re-fetched due to access restrictions, so content is drawn from indexed search summary.

items.title: Gucci Customer Reviews: Quality and Repair Complaints
items.summary: Aggregated customer review page shows recurring complaints about Gucci craftsmanship and after-sales service: glue discoloration within a year of purchase, stitching separating on straps, metal hardware tarnishing with light use, and one case describing a repair item held by the company for five months without resolution.

items.title: TikTok Creators Question Gucci Warranty and Repair Practices
items.summary: Cluster of TikTok posts and comment threads where users compare notes on Gucci's repair and warranty policies after buyers reported paint chipping and hardware issues within months of purchase, with some noting replacement bags exhibited the same durability problems; engagement metrics not visible via search index.

items.title: TikTok Unboxing Wave for Gucci Giglio and Brera Bags
items.summary: Ongoing wave of unboxing and review videos on TikTok centered on the Gucci Giglio hobo and Brera monogram crossbody, positioning both as the platform's current 'trending' Gucci bags; discussion is largely positive/aspirational (aesthetic praise, styling tips) with some price-value debate in comments. Exact view/like counts not retrievable via search snippet.

items.title: How Coty Is Refocusing Its Business With the Gucci Beauty Exit
items.summary: Follow-up analysis on Coty's post-Gucci strategy noting the stock's 14.9 percent one-month gain outpaced the S&P 500 and consumer staples peers even as shares remain down about 55 percent over the past year; piece frames the Gucci exit as forcing Coty to double down on its remaining prestige and mass beauty portfolio.
